scenes.name: Introduction to Blockchain
scenes.narration: Imagine a digital ledger that records every transaction, like a notebook that everyone can see but no one can erase.

scenes.name: Blocks and Chains
scenes.narration: Each page is a block containing a list of transactions. Once full, it's sealed and linked to the previous page, forming a chain.

scenes.name: Decentralization
scenes.narration: Instead of one central notebook, everyone in the network has a copy. This makes it secure and transparent.

scenes.name: How Transactions Work
scenes.narration: When you send money, it's broadcast to the network. Miners verify the transaction and add it to a new block.

scenes.name: Security Through Hashing
scenes.narration: Each block has a unique fingerprint called a hash. Changing any block would break the chain, making fraud easy to detect.

scenes.name: Conclusion
scenes.narration: Blockchain is a transparent, secure, and decentralized way to record transactions. It's the foundation of cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin.
